lede: A look at September 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Joe Locke and Dean Canto, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: NASA
events.desc: NASA's OSIRIS-REx capsule containing samples from the asteroid 101955 Bennu successfully lands back on Earth.

events.title: Thabo Mbeki
events.desc: Thabo Mbeki resigns as president of South Africa.

events.title: Japan Air Lines Flight 472 (1972)
events.desc: Japan Airlines Flight 472 lands at Juhu Aerodrome instead of Santacruz Airport in Bombay, India, overrunning the runway and resulting in 11 injuries.

events.title: Chinchaga fire
events.desc: The eastern United States is covered by a thick haze from the Chinchaga fire in western Canada.

events.title: Honda
events.desc: The Honda Motor Company is founded.

events.title: HMA No. 1
events.desc: His Majesty's Airship No. 1, Britain's first rigid airship, is wrecked by strong winds before her maiden flight at Barrow-in-Furness.

events.title: 1906 Atlanta race massacre
events.desc: Racial tensions exacerbated by rumors lead to the Atlanta Race Riot, further increasing racial segregation.

events.title: Battle of Shiroyama
events.desc: The Battle of Shiroyama is a decisive victory of the Imperial Japanese Army over the Satsuma Rebellion.

events.title: Mexican–American War
events.desc: Mexican–American War: General Zachary Taylor captures Monterrey.

events.title: Judiciary Act of 1789
events.desc: The United States Congress passes the Judiciary Act, creating the office of the Attorney General and federal judiciary system and ordering the composition of the Supreme Court.

births.name: Jaan Teemant
births.line: Estonian lawyer and politician, 7th State Elder of Estonia (died 1941)

deaths.text: Sara Jane Moore, American attempted assassin of Gerald Ford
Charles Reisner, American actor, director, and screenwriter

observances.text: Christian feast day: Our Lady of Mercy and its related observance: La Mercè (Barcelona)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Ransom (Mercedarians)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Walsingham (Church of England)
